<!--
Thank you for your interest in requesting a Magma Starter Kit. These kits, acquired by the Magma Community in 2025, include a Magma core, Magma orchestrator, CPE, and radio units to set up a full private network. The goal is to disseminate the Magma Project throughout the industry and academia. Please provide the following information to help us process your request. Note that sensitive information, such as shipping addresses, should **not** be included in this issue. If approved, you will receive a private form from a `@magmacore.org` email to provide shipping details.
-->

## Request Details

**Destination Country**:  
Please specify the country where the Starter Kit will be shipped.

**Contact Information**:  
Provide your preferred contact method (email or Slack handle) for follow-up communication.

**Purpose of Request**:  
Briefly describe the intended use (e.g., publishing articles citing Magma, training students/personnel, researching features, orchestrating workshops, releasing Magma-powered products, or other contributions to the Magma Project).

**Additional Notes (Optional)**:  
Any other relevant information to support your request, such as prior contributions or planned impact.

<!--
## Process

- Your request will be triaged by the Outreach team.
- The Technical Steering Committee (TSC) will review and approve or deny the request based on the [2025 Starter Kit Allocation and Management Rules](https://lf-magma.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/HOME/pages/77889560/2025+Starter+Kit+-+Allocation+and+Management).
- If approved, you will receive a private form from a `@magmacore.org` email to provide shipping information, which will be shared only with TSC members.

**Note**: Please do not share sensitive information (e.g., addresses) in this issue. All communication regarding sensitive details will be handled securely. Review the full rules linked above for eligibility, allocation criteria, and responsibilities.
-->

<!--
## Key Information

- **Allocation Period**: Approved kits are loaned for 3 months, renewable up to 3 times (maximum 1 year). Renewal requests must be submitted before expiry and are evaluated similarly, with a slight advantage due to no shipping costs.
- **Shipping Costs**: Requesters are responsible for shipping fees, taxes, and paperwork unless the TSC and Outreach committee decide otherwise.
- **Return Policy**: Kits must be returned after the allocation period (or maximum 1 year), with requesters covering return costs unless exempted by the TSC and Outreach committee. Non-compliance may result in sanctions (e.g., Linux Foundation good standing policy, debt registration, litigation).
- **Keeping the Kit**: Requesters may keep the kit by donating its nominal value (last round’s acquisition cost increased by 10%) to the Magma Project.
-->
